themes.name: AI infrastructure & semis
themes.thesis: Hyperscaler + sovereign AI capex flows to the compute, networking, memory, power and cooling toll-takers that every model build must pay, regardless of which lab wins.
themes.notes: The obvious concentration cluster — every layer rides ONE hyperscaler-capex line. Flag in every Director run; <=3 names / <=30% weight binds hardest here.

themes.name: Energy transition & grid
themes.thesis: Electrification + AI-load growth force a decade of grid equipment, generation, storage and nuclear-fuel spending; the equipment and infrastructure toll-takers get paid through every policy zigzag.
themes.notes: Watch the hidden overlap with ai_infrastructure via data-center power (VRT/GEV class names can carry both themes — that is allowed but counts toward BOTH exposure caps).

themes.name: Robotics & automation
themes.thesis: Labor scarcity + reshoring push factories, warehouses and surgery toward robots; the motion-control, vision and surgical-platform toll-takers monetize every deployment wave.
themes.notes: Industrial names here are demand-cycle cyclicals — the Director must separate secular penetration growth from a capex-cycle bounce.

themes.name: Genomics & bio tools
themes.thesis: Sequencing, bioprocessing and lab automation are the picks-and-shovels of biology — paid per experiment and per batch, independent of which therapy wins.
themes.notes: TOOLS, not therapies: no clinical-stage biotech, no PDUFA binaries. A name whose revenue is mostly drug sales maps low-confidence here.

themes.name: Defense tech
themes.thesis: Rearmament budgets are shifting from legacy platforms to C4ISR, drones/counter-drone, space-based ISR and munitions modernization — the software-defined and attritable-systems suppliers grow much faster than the primes.
themes.notes: Legacy primes (maturing s-curve, flat growth) belong to the value book, not here — the Sonnet pass should mark them maturing/low-confidence for this theme.

themes.name: Fintech rails & market infrastructure
themes.thesis: Payment rails, exchanges, market-data and banking software take a toll on transaction and trading volume growth — asset-light compounding without credit risk.
themes.notes: Toll-takers, NOT lenders: names whose economics are a credit book (BNPL balance-sheet lenders, banks) map low-confidence. Financial - Credit Services is included ONLY to catch network/processor names FMP files there (e.g. V/MA class); the Sonnet pass must drop actual lenders.

themes.name: Space economy
themes.thesis: Falling launch costs unlock satellite comms, ground-segment and space-hardware demand; the launch-adjacent suppliers and connectivity toll-takers monetize every constellation, civil or military.
themes.notes: The profitability gates do real work here — most pure-play space names are pre-FCF and must NOT be forced through; an empty or 1-2 name theme is an acceptable, honest outcome.
